articles.title: KEYNOTE, FREE
articles.summary: Salesforce TDX [4] starts tomorrow and the virtual experience is completely free. Join sessions on AI agents, APIs, vibe coding, and platform tooling. Get virtual hands-on training to learn to ship faster, automate workflows, and scale apps natively on Agentforce and Slack. Register and build your agenda [4] from anywhere

articles.title: A BUG ON THE DARK SIDE OF THE MOON
articles.summary: JUXT used its spec tool, Allium, to find a critical four-byte omission in the Apollo Guidance Computer code that failed to release a gyroscope resource lock during a mid-torque "cage" event. This shows the power of behavioral specification in surfacing deep-seated bugs that thousands of people missed for decades.

articles.title: WHAT WE LEARNED BUILDING A RUST RUNTIME FOR TYPESCRIPT
articles.summary: Encore developed a 67,000-line Rust runtime for TypeScript to overcome Node.js's single-threaded limitations and establish a multi-language core for future extensibility. By offloading infrastructure concerns and integrating tightly with Node.js via N-API, this architecture achieves up to 9x higher throughput and 80% less latency compared to other frameworks.

articles.title: MOVING RAILWAY'S FRONTEND OFF NEXT.JS
articles.summary: Railway migrated its production frontend from Next.js to Vite and TanStack Router to resolve slow build times and better support its client-side architecture. The transition of over 200 routes was completed with zero downtime, resulting in a faster development loop and improved type safety across the application.

articles.title: WRITE LESS CODE, BE MORE RESPONSIBLE
articles.summary: The discourse around AI-assisted programming often advocates for a balanced approach: using AI for tedious tasks while developers personally craft the enjoyable parts to guarantee quality. However, there are growing concerns about a potential decline in project quality and maintainability due to vibe-coded applications.

articles.title: THE ECONOMICS OF SOFTWARE TEAMS: WHY MOST ENGINEERING ORGANIZATIONS ARE FLYING BLIND
articles.summary: Most organizations "fly blind" regarding their software teams' economics because they don't have the financial visibility to understand both their substantial costs and the value they must generate to be truly viable.

articles.title: BECOME AI-FIRST IN 10 WEEKS OF SUSTAINED EXECUTION. ARE YOU READY?
articles.summary: Gauntlet [10] is the fellowship that makes you AI-first. Access frontier models at the Austin intensive and deliver against real constraints and real deadlines. Graduates receive starting compensation of $200-950k after shipping prod-ready AI systems for 10 weeks. 300+ first-round interviews already secured. Apply for Cohort 5

articles.title: GITHUB STACKED PRS
articles.summary: GitHub Stacked PRs let developers break large code changes into small, interdependent pull requests, simplifying code reviews and speeding up the merge process. The system integrates natively with GitHub, providing a UI and a gh stack CLI for local management, and allows merging an entire stack with a single click.

articles.title: FUSEJS
articles.summary: Fuse.js is a lightweight, zero-dependency JavaScript library that provides powerful fuzzy-searching capabilities for both the browser and server-side environments. Using the Bitap algorithm, it offers typo-tolerant matching and advanced features like weighted keys, logical operators, and tokenized searching within small-to-medium datasets.

articles.title: HOW WE OPTIMIZED AGENT DEVICE FOR MOBILE APP AUTOMATION
articles.summary: Callstack developed the “agent-device” CLI to enable AI agents to control mobile devices for automated testing, achieving over 50% reduction in LLM token usage by replacing screenshots with trimmed, text-based accessibility-tree snapshots. This method aggressively trims the mobile UI hierarchy to only visible elements, improving the signal-to-noise ratio for the agents and keeping the context window useful longer.

articles.title: THE UPHILL CLIMB OF MAKING DIFF LINES PERFORMANT
articles.summary: GitHub's “Files changed” tab struggled with large pull requests because each diff line contained 8+ React components, leading a 10k-line diff to consume 1GB of heap and create over 400k DOM nodes. The team solved this by rewriting the feature to use only two components per line, flattening state, and adding virtualization, resulting in a 78% cut in INP and a 50% reduction in memory.

articles.title: BUILDING A CLI FOR ALL OF CLOUDFLARE
articles.summary: Cloudflare is rebuilding its Wrangler CLI into a unified "cf" tool that provides consistent infrastructure-as-code management across its entire catalog of over 100 products. This new TypeScript-based architecture guarantees synchronization between CLIs and SDKs.

articles.title: TEAMS ARE GOING AI-NATIVE. HOW ARE YOU DOING IT?
articles.summary: Augment Code has been documenting how they're transforming their engineering team [17] to be AI-native. They're looking to hear from other engineering leaders going through the same shift via a quick 5-min survey [18].

articles.title: WHAT THE AXIOS SUPPLY CHAIN ATTACK REVEALED ABOUT LOCKFILES AND PNPM 10
articles.summary: The Axios supply chain attack showed the importance of lockfiles for making sure predictable dependency versions and pnpm v10's safeguards for controlling install-time script execution to mitigate similar supply chain risks.

articles.title: I JUST WANT SIMPLE S3
articles.summary: The author, looking for a simple, fast, and reliable S3 solution for basic needs, reviews several options like Minio and SeaweedFS, ultimately finding success with Versity GW due to its excellent performance when using a local filesystem backend.

articles.title: HOW TO MAKE FIREFOX BUILDS 17% FASTER
articles.summary: buildcache's Lua plugin system can now cache Firefox's Python-based WebIDL code generation step, speeding up warm builds and showing its potential for optimizing other deterministic build processes.

articles.title: YOUR STARTUP IS PROBABLY DEAD ON ARRIVAL
articles.summary: Rapid AI advancements have rendered most existing startup plans and technologies obsolete, so founders must re-evaluate their strategies and pivot to outcome-driven models to survive.
